vehicle.label: Tiltrotor VTOL Hybrid
vehicle.desc: Fixed-wing VTOL with 2 tilting rotor nacelles, BVLOS long-range capable

parts.label: Center Fuselage Spine
parts.spec: CFRP box beam center fuselage, avionics bay, wing spar socket, front GPS boom mount, belly camera window

parts.label: Main Wing (2-piece)
parts.spec: Straight wing, 4S NACA 2412, dual 15mm CFRP spar, 900mm half-span, 160mm chord, 3-layer CFRP skin

parts.label: Nacelle Assembly x 2
parts.spec: Tilting nacelle assembly, 0-90deg tilt axis, 6309 bearing set, servo-driven worm gear, motor and ESC pocket

parts.label: Tilt Servo + Linkage x 2
parts.spec: 25kg-cm DS3218 servo per nacelle, titanium pushrod, dual ball joints, tilt range 0-93deg, crash-tolerant

parts.label: V-Tail x 2 (Ruddervator)
parts.spec: +-35deg V-tail ruddervator pair, MG996R servo each, 30deg dihedral, CFRP sandwich panel, hinge piano wire

parts.label: Landing Gear (Tri)
parts.spec: Tricycle landing gear, spring-steel skid arms, 55mm dia wheels, CG at 1/3 chord, 200mm wheelbase

parts.label: Battery Bay (Slide-In)
parts.spec: Belly slide-in battery bay, 6S 8000mAh Li-ion pack, Velcro + strap dual retention, CG position 28% MAC

parts.label: Payload Nose Bay
parts.spec: Interchangeable nose payload bay, Day/IR camera gimbal or survey sensor, 80x60x50mm, 300g payload limit

parts.label: Tilt-Axis Harness + Datum Service Bay
parts.spec: Tiltrotor integration_service module with tilt-axis harness_channel, slack_loop, interface_datum, service_hatch, connector keying, and nacelle-to-wing load-path inspection access

risk.desc: Potential thermal stress due to high heat generation in the avionics bay.
risk.mit: Implement thermal management solutions such as heat sinks and cooling ducts.

risk.desc: Risk of mechanical failure at the wing spar socket under high load.
risk.mit: Increase material thickness and reinforce the socket area with additional ribs.

risk.desc: Service access may be limited due to complex internal geometry.
risk.mit: Design service access panels with clear labeling and easy removal.

risk.desc: Potential for water ingress through the belly camera window.
risk.mit: Use waterproof seals and ensure proper drainage paths.

risk.desc: Risk of electrical interference from the front GPS boom mount.
risk.mit: Ensure proper shielding and grounding of the GPS mount.

verify.rule: All required fields are present in the blueprint.
The mass estimate is within a reasonable range for the subsystem.
Each P0 internal_feature_target appears as a part_tree child and geometry_ops id or target.
Each interface has a named neighboring subsystem label and clear joint family.
Critical joints have visible locking or preload evidence.
Boundary conditions are specified with fixture points, thermal zones, and pressure faces.

risk.desc: The wing spar may not be able to handle the full load from the nacelle rotor without additional reinforcement.
risk.mit: Add leading/trailing edge reinforcements to the wing spar.

risk.desc: The thermal cycling tests may not fully simulate the high-altitude environment.
risk.mit: Include additional thermal cycling tests at simulated high-altitude conditions.

risk.desc: The service access validation may not account for the complexity of removing the nacelle motor and servo.
risk.mit: Conduct a detailed service access validation with mockup parts.

risk.desc: The press-fit connection for the nacelle rotor may require additional preload evidence.
risk.mit: Add visible locking or preload evidence to the press-fit connection.

risk.desc: The snap-fit connection for the V-tail may need additional clearance to ensure proper fit.
risk.mit: Adjust the clearance for the snap-fit connection to ensure a proper fit.

risk.desc: Potential misalignment during assembly due to complex press-fit and bolted interfaces.
risk.mit: Implement precise alignment tools and detailed assembly instructions.

risk.desc: Cooling fins may not provide sufficient heat dissipation during high-thrust operations.
risk.mit: Increase the surface area of cooling fins or add additional cooling mechanisms.

risk.desc: Access covers may not provide adequate service access for all components.
risk.mit: Review and optimize access cover design to ensure full component access.

risk.desc: Press-fit and bolted interfaces may require additional torque checks for long-term durability.
risk.mit: Include periodic torque checks in maintenance procedures.

risk.desc: Service access may be compromised during high-altitude operations due to pressurization.
risk.mit: Ensure all seals and access points are rated for high-altitude operations.

_subsystem_plan.subsystem_role: Tilt axis with bearing seats, servo linkage, rotation clearance arc

physics_paths.description: Rotation of the tilt axis drives the ball joint through the titanium pushrod
Rotor thrust loads are transferred through the bearing seats to the wing structure
Heat generated by the servo is dissipated through cooling fins
Aerospace-grade access covers provide maintenance access to the servo and linkage
Atmospheric pressure is maintained by the enclosure seal

integration_opportunities.description: Integration of cooling fins with the enclosure for optimal heat dissipation
Enhanced structural integrity of the bearing seat for rotor thrust loads
Design of access covers for easy maintenance and inspection

risk.desc: The harness routing through the tilt axis may be susceptible to mechanical stress during rotation.
risk.mit: Implement strain relief features and ensure routing respects minimum bend radius.

risk.desc: The service hatch may not be fully sealed against environmental conditions at high altitudes.
risk.mit: Add sealing gaskets and perform pressure tests to ensure airtightness.

risk.desc: The interface flange may not provide sufficient clearance for thermal expansion.
risk.mit: Increase clearance between mating parts to accommodate thermal expansion.

risk.desc: The fastener pattern may not provide adequate preload for high-load joints.
risk.mit: Use appropriate washers and specify preload torque values.

risk.desc: The rib structure may not provide sufficient stiffness for the subsystem.
risk.mit: Increase rib thickness or add additional ribs to improve stiffness.
